name: Content Business
description: Content businesses — newsletters, courses, media, creative studios, and publishing.

idea_prompts.user: Who is the specific reader, viewer, or learner — what is their existing information diet, where do they currently spend attention, and what makes them a distinct audience segment rather than a broad demographic?
idea_prompts.problem: What is the information gap, signal-to-noise problem, or unmet creative need this content addresses — and why do existing publications, courses, or media fail to serve this audience well?
idea_prompts.solution: What is the primary content format and publishing cadence — written, audio, video, or course — and what is the editorial point of view that makes this unmistakably yours rather than interchangeable with competitors?
idea_prompts.success: In 18 months, what does a healthy content business look like — subscriber count, revenue per subscriber, renewal rate, or a licensing or syndication deal that validates the audience relationship?
idea_prompts.risks: What is your riskiest assumption about distribution channel and monetization model — and what happens to the business if the primary platform (email, YouTube, LinkedIn, a marketplace) changes its algorithm or terms?
